sections.paragraph:
οἰκτίρμων, ον (οἰκτείρω; Gorgias, Palam. 32 Blass; Theocr. 15, 75; Anth. Pal. 7, 359, 1; Sb 3923 οἰκτείρμων; LXX; TestJud 19:3; JosAs ch. 11 cod. A [p. 54, 7 Bat.]; Anth. Pal. 364) pert. to being concerned about another’s unfortunate state or misery, merciful, compassionate of God (so almost always LXX and, in addition, always combined w. ἐλεήμων; Did., Gen. 83, 5 οἰκ. θεός; w. ἀγαθός and χρηστός Theoph. Ant. 1, 3 [p. 62, 23]) w. πολύσπλαγχνος Js 5:11. W. εὐεργετικός 1 Cl 23:1. W. ἐλεήμων 60:1.—Of humans also (Memnon Hist. [I b.c./I a.d.]: 434 Fgm. 1, 3, 2 Jac.; Ps 108:12; La 4:10) Lk 6:36.—DELG s.v. οἶκτο. TW.

Theocr Theocr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

  1. ch. = chapter(s)

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Memnon Memnon (of Heracleon?), I b.c.–I? a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 700
